extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by r2 (IF elderly, priority 50), and rec_drug_b is set true only by r4 (IF elderly, priority 50). Both rules trigger on the identical condition elderly=true, and no other rule competes for or resets either output (r1, r3, r5 only affect rec_drug_c). Therefore any input with elderly=true makes both r2 and r4 fire, setting rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously. Priorities and defaults do not prevent this since the two outputs are independent and each is uniquely driven by elderly. Counterexample: elderly=true (others false) yields rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true, violating mutual exclusion.
